title: elegia recta
intro: Elegia recta, also known as African boxwood or umbrella bush, is a shrub native to South Africa, characterized by its small, rounded leaves and distinctive umbrella-shaped growth habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. Elegia recta has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses.
